description: Default per-item iteration: fetch a list and post one Slack message per item. The agent should wire source straight through (Split Out if needed) without Loop Over Items / Split In Batches.

conversation.text: Every hour, fetch open tasks from GET https://api.example.com/tasks and post each task's title and assignee to Slack channel #ops-tasks — one message per task. The API returns a JSON envelope {tasks: [...]}, so split that into individual items before posting.

outcomeExpectations: The workflow fetches tasks and posts to Slack once per task (per-item downstream processing), not a single combined message for all tasks.
The agent did NOT add a Loop Over Items / Split In Batches node — default item iteration is sufficient for one Slack post per task.

executionScenarios.name: three-tasks-three-messages
executionScenarios.description: API returns three tasks in envelope; Slack runs three times
executionScenarios.dataSetup: GET https://api.example.com/tasks returns a single JSON object { "tasks": [{ "title": "Fix login", "assignee": "Alex" }, { "title": "Update docs", "assignee": "Sam" }, { "title": "Ship release", "assignee": "Jordan" }] } — NOT a bare array at the top level. Each Slack postMessage call must return a distinct success response with a different ts value: first { "ok": true, "ts": "1700000000.0001" }, second { "ok": true, "ts": "1700000000.0002" }, third { "ok": true, "ts": "1700000000.0003" }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Judge from intercepted outbound requests in the execution trace: Slack postMessage (or equivalent HTTP post to a Slack API) is called three times — once per task — and each message includes that task's title and assignee (e.g. 'Fix login' and 'Alex'). A workflow that posts one combined message for all tasks FAILS. A workflow that never splits the tasks envelope and posts zero or one time FAILS.

executionScenarios.name: empty-task-list
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow completes without errors. Slack postMessage is not invoked (or invoked zero times).
